methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Collaborative Robotics, founded by Brad Porter, is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company seeking valuation guidance following its $500M+ post-money valuation and $150M+ cumulative fundraising. The peer set comprises five same-stage private comparables in the robotics sector, ranging from $300M to $10.0B in valuation, with one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $500M last post-money valuation as a base and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a $950M valuation. Peer-median analysis across five robotics firms at Series B stage (1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Fairmat, and GrayMatter Robotics) yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in robotics applications and commercialization progress within the cohort.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $808M to $1.9B, with a median of $1.3B. The $500M current valuation sits at the lower bound of this analysis, suggesting potential upside subject to execution risk. Key uncertainties include the company's relative product maturity and market traction versus peers, particularly given the extreme outlier valuation of 1X Technologies at $10.0B, and the limited public comparables available for cross-validation in this nascent sector.
